key_uncertainties: differentiation versus CocoCay and Paradise Island
2024-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Introduced as the newest addition to the private-destinations portfolio with a 2026 target opening, framed as enhancing the guest beach-day experience alongside CocoCay and Paradise Island.

status_delta.detail: first quarter this asset appears in the lifecycle record

2024-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: Cited as reaching important milestones in parallel with Royal Beach Club Paradise Island (2025 opening), with a 2026 Cozumel opening reaffirmed.

key_uncertainties: interaction with Perfect Day Mexico positioning
Gulf Coast and drive-market sourcing strategy
2024-Q3.mgmt_statement_summary: Positioned alongside Perfect Day Mexico (2027) as anchors of an expanded private-destination collection, with Cozumel highlighted as a lever for lower-cost flight-cruise itineraries from the Gulf Coast and other drive markets.

key_uncertainties: share of 2025 non-ship capex attributable to Cozumel
2024-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Identified as part of $1.6B 2025 non-ship capital, alongside the Costa Maya port acquisition and Paradise Island opening; flagged as a key contributor to lifting Royal Caribbean private-destination penetration to roughly 90% by 2027.

2025-Q2.mgmt_statement_summary: Opening described as end of 2026, with Costa Maya also coming online in 2026 and Perfect Day Mexico following in late 2027 as part of the broader land-based destination expansion.

key_uncertainties: 2026 non-ship capex split between Santorini, Cozumel, and Perfect Day Mexico
timing slippage versus the prior end-of-2026 plan
2025-Q4.mgmt_statement_summary: Grouped with Santorini Beach Club and Perfect Day Mexico as the primary destinations driving $1.8B of 2026 non-ship capex; paired with Perfect Day Mexico as the assets that lift private-destination guest penetration to 90% by 2028.

key_uncertainties: early-2028 opening credibility after the slip from end-2026
incremental 2027 carry costs with no revenue contribution
ramp cadence relative to Perfect Day Mexico
2026-Q1.mgmt_statement_summary: Opening explicitly pushed to early 2028, paired with Perfect Day Mexico (late 2027 opening, ramping into early 2028) and Costa Maya; jointly described as the centerpiece of the Western Caribbean strategy alongside Icon Class deployment.

asked_by_history.framing: Differentiation of Cozumel versus CocoCay and Paradise Island from a marketing and guest-segmentation perspective, including by ship class
Inorganic 2026 yield contribution from new assets, noting Cozumel was not expected to contribute in 2026 while Paradise Island would

primary_framings_observed: Differentiation and guest segmentation versus other private destinations
Inorganic contribution timing to yield growth
Sequencing with Paradise Island, Costa Maya, and Perfect Day Mexico in the Western Caribbean buildout

currently_open_questions_as_of_2026Q1: Credibility of the revised early-2028 opening after slippage from end-2026
Magnitude of 2026-2027 carry costs and capex with no offsetting revenue
Sizing of Western Caribbean yield uplift attributable specifically to Cozumel versus Perfect Day Mexico
Drive-market and Gulf Coast sourcing impact on Cozumel-itinerary pricing
Capacity, amenity mix, and per-guest unit economics at maturity
Sequencing of revenue contribution in 2028 relative to Perfect Day Mexico's late-2027 opening
